NameFlagsCard.TypeDescription & Constraints
EHDSDischargeReport 0..*EHDSDischargeReportDischarge Report model
header O1..1BaseDocument header
ObligationsActor
SHALL:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:processConsumer
subject O1..1EHDSPatientPatient/subject information
ObligationsActor
SHALL:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:displayConsumer
SHALL:processConsumer
identifier O1..*IdentifierUnique identifier of the document
ObligationsActor
SHALL:able-to-populateProducer
SHOULD:displayConsumer
SHALL:processConsumer
author[x] O1..*EHDSHealthProfessional, EHDSOrganisation, EHDSDeviceThe author of the report.
ObligationsActor
SHALL:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:displayConsumer
date O1..1dateTimeDate of finalising/issuing the document
ObligationsActor
SHALL:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:displayConsumer
status O1..1CodeableConceptStatus of the document
ObligationsActor
SHALL:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:displayConsumer
language O0..1CodeableConceptLanguage in which the resource is written. Language is expressed by the IETF language tag.
ObligationsActor
SHALL:able-to-populateProducer
documentType O1..1CodeableConceptType of the document (e.g. '34105-7 Hospital discharge summary)
ObligationsActor
SHALL:able-to-populateProducer
documentTitle O1..1stringTitle of the document, possibly autogenerated from the structured fields (e.g. 'Hospital discharge report. Jane Green. 07.10.2025-10.10.2025')
ObligationsActor
SHALL:able-to-populateProducer
period O0..1PeriodTime of service that is being documented (typically the same as the time/period of encounter)
ObligationsActor
SHALL:able-to-populateProducer
attestation O0..*BaseDocument attestation details
ObligationsActor
SHALL:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:displayConsumer
attester[x] O1..1EHDSHealthProfessional, EHDSDeviceAttester who validated the document
ObligationsActor
SHALL:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:displayConsumer
datetime O1..1dateTimeDate and time of the approval of the document by Attester
ObligationsActor
SHALL:able-to-populateProducer
legalAuthentication O0..*BaseDocument legal authentication details
ObligationsActor
SHALL:able-to-populateProducer
legalAuthenticator[x] O1..1EHDSHealthProfessional, EHDSOrganisationThe person or organisation taking responsibility for the content of the document.
ObligationsActor
SHALL:able-to-populateProducer
datetime O1..1dateTimeDate and time when the document was authorised
ObligationsActor
SHALL:able-to-populateProducer
eventType O0..*CodeableConceptCategorisation of the event covered by the document (e.g. laboratory study types, imaging study types including modality, etc.). Selection of such tags or labels depends on the use case and agreement between data sharing parties. This meta-data element serves primarily for searching and filtering purposes.
ObligationsActor
SHALL:able-to-populateProducer
serviceSpecialty O0..*CodeableConceptAdditional details about where the content was created (e.g. clinical specialty)
ObligationsActor
SHALL:able-to-populateProducer
SHOULD:displayConsumer
custodian O0..1EHDSOrganisationOrganisation that is in charge of maintaining the document/report.
ObligationsActor
SHALL:able-to-populateProducer
presentedForm O0..*EHDSAttachmentA narrative easy-to-read representation of the full data set, e.g. PDF-version of a document
ObligationsActor
SHALL:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:processConsumer
body O0..1BaseStructured body of the discharge report document
ObligationsActor
SHALL:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:processConsumer
alerts O0..1BaseSection: Alerts.
ObligationsActor
SHOULD:able-to-populateProducer
SHOULD:processConsumer
generatedNarrative O0..1stringNarrative, potentially formatted, content of the whole section.
ObligationsActor
SHOULD:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:processConsumer
medicalAlert O0..*EHDSAlertDescription of medical alerts in textual format: any clinical information that is imperative to know so that the life or health of the patient does not come under threat.
ObligationsActor
SHOULD:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:processConsumer
encounterInformation O1..1BaseSection: Encounter information.
ObligationsActor
SHALL:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:processConsumer
generatedNarrative O0..1stringNarrative, potentially formatted, content of the whole section.
ObligationsActor
SHALL:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:processConsumer
encounter O0..1EHDSEncounterEncounter information
ObligationsActor
SHALL:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:processConsumer
note O0..1stringFree text notes by the health professional
ObligationsActor
SHOULD:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:processConsumer
admissionEvaluation O0..1BaseSection: Admission evaluation. Admission evaluation section should be reported only if it is relevant to ensure continuity of care.
ObligationsActor
SHALL:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:processConsumer
generatedNarrative O0..1stringNarrative, potentially formatted, content of the whole section.
ObligationsActor
SHOULD:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:processConsumer
objectiveFindings O0..*EHDSObservationObjective findings, such as anthropometric measurements, vital signs, or objective anatomical findings of physical examination
ObligationsActor
SHOULD:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:processConsumer
functionalStatus[x] O0..*EHDSCondition, EHDSObservationAn individual's ability to perform normal daily activities required to meet basic needs, fulfil usual roles and maintain health and well-being
ObligationsActor
SHOULD:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:processConsumer
note O0..1stringFree text notes by the evaluation performer
ObligationsActor
SHOULD:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:processConsumer
patientHistory O0..1BaseSection: Patient health history (anamnesis) containing only information that is highly relevant for this specific episode of care. The model is designed with the expectation that patient summary is available.
ObligationsActor
SHOULD:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:processConsumer
generatedNarrative O0..1stringNarrative, potentially formatted, content of the whole section. In case the section includes structured information, it should also be included in generatedNarrative.
ObligationsActor
SHOULD:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:processConsumer
pastProblems O0..*EHDSConditionA list of conditions of a patient that the patient suffered in the past or still suffers. Unlike diagnostic summary, medical history is not only a list of problems, but could contain broader description of the condition and its progress, details about treatment including medication and patient response to treatment. Past problem section (unlike the same section of the patient summary) should include only conditions that are important for continuity of care. This section, if provided, complements the diagnostic summary section of the discharge report.
ObligationsActor
SHOULD:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:processConsumer
devicesAndImplants O0..*EHDSDeviceUseDevices and implants in patient anamnesis.
ObligationsActor
SHOULD:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:processConsumer
procedures O0..*EHDSProcedureProcedures in patient anamnesis.
ObligationsActor
SHOULD:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:processConsumer
note O0..1stringFree text notes by the health professional
ObligationsActor
SHOULD:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:processConsumer
courseOfEncounter O1..1BaseCourse of inpatient or outpatient encounter.
ObligationsActor
SHALL:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:processConsumer
generatedNarrative O0..1stringNarrative, potentially formatted, content of the whole section.
ObligationsActor
SHOULD:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:processConsumer
diagnosticSummary O0..*EHDSConditionProblems that were treated or affected provisioning of care (diagnostics, therapy, nursing, monitoring) during the encounter. At least one treated problem should be marked. Other problems are recorded only if they are important for continuity of care (after discharge).
ObligationsActor
SHOULD:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:processConsumer
procedures O0..*EHDSProcedureProcedures performed during encounter which are significant for continuity of care, e.g. surgeries and other instrumental interventions (endoscopic, intravascular), chemotherapy, radiotherapy, purification methods (dialysis, hemoperfusion), circulation support methods (counterpulsation, etc.), administration of blood derivatives or others. Diagnostic procedures are typically captured as observation results and not repeated here.
ObligationsActor
SHOULD:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:processConsumer
medicalDevicesAndImplants O0..*EHDSDeviceUseMedical devices (including implants) whose use was started, stopped, or otherwise modified during the encounter.
ObligationsActor
SHOULD:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:processConsumer
pharmacotherapy O0..*EHDSMedicationUseSignificant medication treatments during encounter. The content of this section may be repeated in the medication summary for full overview of patient's medications.
ObligationsActor
SHOULD:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:processConsumer
testResults[x] O0..*EHDSObservation, EHDSLaboratoryObservationSignificant medical test results of functional, diagnostic (including laboratory), and imaging examinations performed during encounter. This may include orders for which the results have not yet arrived.
ObligationsActor
SHOULD:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:processConsumer
note O0..1stringFree text notes by the health professional
ObligationsActor
SHOULD:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:processConsumer
dischargeDetails O0..1BaseDischarge details
ObligationsActor
SHOULD:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:processConsumer
generatedNarrative O0..1stringNarrative, potentially formatted, content of the whole section.
ObligationsActor
SHOULD:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:processConsumer
objectiveFindings O0..*EHDSObservationObjective findings, such as anthropometric measurements, vital signs, or objective anatomical findings of physical examination
ObligationsActor
SHOULD:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:processConsumer
functionalStatus[x] O0..*EHDSCondition, EHDSObservationAn individual's ability to perform normal daily activities required to meet basic needs, fulfil usual roles and maintain health and well-being
ObligationsActor
SHOULD:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:processConsumer
note O0..1stringFree text notes by the health professional
ObligationsActor
SHOULD:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:processConsumer
medicationSummary O0..1BaseSummary information on the medication recommended for the period after discharge, indicating whether the medication is changed or newly started. Compared to previous practices, the overview is supplemented with medication that has been discontinued.
ObligationsActor
SHALL:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:processConsumer
generatedNarrative O0..1stringNarrative, potentially formatted, content of the whole section.
ObligationsActor
SHOULD:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:processConsumer
medicationUse O0..*EHDSMedicationUseDetails about medication and dosage
ObligationsActor
SHALL:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:processConsumer
note O0..1stringFree text notes by the health professional
ObligationsActor
SHOULD:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:processConsumer
carePlan O0..1BaseCare plan and other recommendations after discharge.
ObligationsActor
SHOULD:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:processConsumer
generatedNarrative O0..1stringNarrative, potentially formatted, content of the whole section.
ObligationsActor
SHOULD:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:processConsumer
otherRecommendations O0..1stringOther recommendations (advice) after discharge. E.g., recommendation to suggest hip replacement, reduce number of cigarettes, stop smoking, increase physical exercises, etc.
ObligationsActor
SHOULD:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:processConsumer
synthesis O0..1stringClinical synthesis (e.g. description of reasons and course of encounter) clustered by managed conditions. Clinical synthesis may include clinical reasoning (differential diagnostics, explanation of clinical context) in clinically complex conditions.
ObligationsActor
SHOULD:able-to-populateProducer
SHALL:processConsumer

Documentation for this format

name: EHDSDischargeReportObligations
title: Discharge Report Obligations
description: Obligations for the logical model of discharge report.

element.short: Discharge Report model
element.definition: A generic, flexible model for any kind of discharge report. Different types of encounters may require adding relevant sections and elements, or omitting irrelevant ones, depending on their data needs.

extension.valueMarkdown: A common document header is necessary to ensure that the document can be unambiguously identified, associated with a patient, and processed in an exchange context. Systems claiming conformance must be capable of populating this metadata for every document instance.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process document header information.

element.short: Document header
element.definition: Basic metadata for this information

extension.valueMarkdown: EHR systems SHALL provide information about the subject of care.
EHR system SHALL render information about the subject of care in such a way that unambiguous assignment to the care subject is preserved.
Information about the subject of care SHALL be processed by the EHR system in such a way that the identity of the subject of care cannot be confused.

element.short: Patient/subject information
element.definition: Patient/subject information

element.short: Unique identifier of the document
element.definition: Business identifier for the object, unique within its system. Supporting disambiguation between different contexts (systems/countries).

element.short: The author of the report.
element.definition: The author of the report.

element.short: Date of finalising/issuing the document
element.definition: Date and optionally time of authoring/issuing

element.short: Status of the document
element.definition: Status of the document

element.short: Source of information (e.g. patient, patient's proxy). Used in case the patient has authored the information or the author has submitted patient-stated information.
element.definition: Source of information (e.g. patient, patient's proxy). Used in case the patient has authored the information or the author has submitted patient-stated information.

element.short: Language in which the resource is written. Language is expressed by the IETF language tag.
element.definition: Language in which the resource is written. Language is expressed by the IETF language tag.

element.short: Type of the document (e.g. '34105-7 Hospital discharge summary)
element.definition: Type of the document (e.g. '34105-7 Hospital discharge summary)

element.short: Title of the document, possibly autogenerated from the structured fields (e.g. 'Hospital discharge report. Jane Green. 07.10.2025-10.10.2025')
element.definition: Title of the document, possibly autogenerated from the structured fields (e.g. 'Hospital discharge report. Jane Green. 07.10.2025-10.10.2025')

element.short: Time of service that is being documented (typically the same as the time/period of encounter)
element.definition: Time of service that is being documented

element.short: Business version of the document. The exact algorithm for versioning is decided by the producer/custodian
element.definition: Business version of the document. The exact algorithm for versioning is decided by the producer/custodian

element.short: Document attestation details
element.definition: Document attestation details

element.short: Attester who validated the document
element.definition: Attester who validated the document

element.short: Date and time of the approval of the document by Attester
element.definition: Date and time of the approval of the document by Attester

element.short: Document legal authentication details
element.definition: Document legal authentication details

element.short: The person or organisation taking responsibility for the content of the document.
element.definition: The person or organisation taking responsibility for the content of the document.

element.short: Date and time when the document was authorised
element.definition: Date and time when the document was authorised

element.short: Categorisation of the event covered by the document (e.g. laboratory study types, imaging study types including modality, etc.). Selection of such tags or labels depends on the use case and agreement between data sharing parties. This meta-data element serves primarily for searching and filtering purposes.
element.definition: Categorisation of the event covered by the document (e.g. laboratory study types, imaging study types including modality, etc.). Selection of such tags or labels depends on the use case and agreement between data sharing parties. This meta-data element serves primarily for searching and filtering purposes.

element.short: Additional details about where the content was created (e.g. clinical specialty)
element.definition: Additional details about where the content was created (e.g. clinical specialty)

element.short: Organisation that is in charge of maintaining the document/report.
element.definition: Organisation that is in charge of maintaining the document/report.

extension.valueMarkdown: Systems claiming DR conformance shall demonstrate the capability to generate a human-readable representation of the document when required by the exchange context. This obligation applies to capability, not to population in every instance.
EHR systems that consume DR report SHALL be able to process the human readable version of the report.

element.short: A narrative easy-to-read representation of the full data set, e.g. PDF-version of a document
element.definition: A narrative easy-to-read representation of the full data set, e.g. PDF-version of a document

element.short: Intended recipient(s) of the information, i.e. a person or organisation that should be notified or be aware of the content. This element is used to indicate explicit communication intent and does not represent routine storage or passive availability of information (e.g. in portals). Listing an intended recipient does not create an assignment or responsibility.
element.definition: Intended recipient(s) of the information, i.e. a person or organisation that should be notified or be aware of the content. This element is used to indicate explicit communication intent and does not represent routine storage or passive availability of information (e.g. in portals). Listing an intended recipient does not create an assignment or responsibility.

extension.valueMarkdown: Each EHR system that is a producer of a discharge report SHALL be able to provide structured information about the report body. This obligation applies to capability, not to population in every instance.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process structured body information.

element.short: Structured body of the discharge report document
element.definition: Structured body of the discharge report document

extension.valueMarkdown: Alerts may not be present in all encounters, but the document structure must support their representation. Systems must be capable of populating and processing this section when alerts are present; omission is acceptable where permanently out of scope and justified during conformance testing.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process the alerts section when it is present in the document.

element.short: Section: Alerts.
element.definition: Information about substantial alerts or warnings (including allergies) that health professionals should be aware of.

extension.valueMarkdown: Narrative representation supports immediate clinical understanding of alerts. Systems must be capable of populating this narrative; omission is acceptable where permanently out of scope and justified during conformance testing.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process the generatedNarrative element of the alerts section when it is present in the document.

element.short: Narrative, potentially formatted, content of the whole section.
element.definition: Narrative, potentially formatted, content of the whole section.

extension.valueMarkdown: Structured or textual alert entries represent clinically critical information when present. Systems must be capable of populating these entries; omission is acceptable where permanently out of scope and justified during conformance testing.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process the medicalAlert element of the alerts section when it is present in the document.

element.short: Description of medical alerts in textual format: any clinical information that is imperative to know so that the life or health of the patient does not come under threat.
element.definition: Description of medical alerts in textual format: any clinical information that is imperative to know so that the life or health of the patient does not come under threat.

extension.valueMarkdown: Encounter context is essential for interpreting all clinical content in the document. Systems claiming conformance must be capable of populating this section for every document instance.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process structured information about the encounter.

element.short: Section: Encounter information.
element.definition: Section: Encounter information.

extension.valueMarkdown: A narrative description of the encounter context is necessary to ensure human interpretability. Systems must be capable of populating this narrative in every document instance.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process the generatedNarrative element of the encounterInformation section.

element.short: Narrative, potentially formatted, content of the whole section.
element.definition: Narrative, potentially formatted, content of the whole section.

extension.valueMarkdown: A structured encounter reference supports linkage and reuse where available. Systems must be capable of populating this reference; omission is acceptable where permanently out of scope and justified during conformance testing.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process the encounter element of the encounterInformation section.

element.short: Encounter information
element.definition: Encounter information

element.short: Free text notes by the health professional
element.definition: Free text notes by the health professional

extension.valueMarkdown: Admission evaluation does not occur in all encounters but must be representable where applicable. Systems must be capable of populating this section; omission is acceptable where permanently out of scope and justified during conformance testing.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process structured information about the admission evaluation.

element.short: Section: Admission evaluation. Admission evaluation section should be reported only if it is relevant to ensure continuity of care.
element.definition: Section: Admission evaluation. Admission evaluation section should be reported only if it is relevant to ensure continuity of care.

extension.valueMarkdown: Narrative summarisation supports clinical understanding of admission findings. Systems must be capable of populating this narrative; omission is acceptable where permanently out of scope and justified during conformance testing.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process the generatedNarrative element of the admissionEvaluation section.

element.short: Narrative, potentially formatted, content of the whole section.
element.definition: Narrative, potentially formatted, content of the whole section.

extension.valueMarkdown: Objective findings are part of structured clinical assessment when performed. Systems must be capable of populating this container; omission is acceptable where permanently out of scope and justified during conformance testing.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process the objectiveFindings element of the admissionEvaluation section when it is present in the document.

element.short: Objective findings, such as anthropometric measurements, vital signs, or objective anatomical findings of physical examination
element.definition: Objective findings, such as anthropometric measurements, vital signs, or objective anatomical findings of physical examination

extension.valueMarkdown: Functional status assessment is context-dependent but relevant for continuity of care. Systems must be capable of populating this section; omission is acceptable where permanently out of scope and justified during conformance testing.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process the functionalStatus element of the admissionEvaluation section when it is present in the document.

element.short: An individual's ability to perform normal daily activities required to meet basic needs, fulfil usual roles and maintain health and well-being
element.definition: An individual's ability to perform normal daily activities required to meet basic needs, fulfil usual roles and maintain health and well-being

element.short: Free text notes by the evaluation performer
element.definition: Free text notes by the evaluation performer

extension.valueMarkdown: Patient history may not be available or relevant in all care contexts, but systems must be capable of representing this section when such information exists. Omission is acceptable where the capability is permanently out of scope and justified during conformance testing.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process structured information about patient history.

element.short: Section: Patient health history (anamnesis) containing only information that is highly relevant for this specific episode of care. The model is designed with the expectation that patient summary is available.
element.definition: Section: Patient health history (anamnesis) containing only information that is highly relevant for this specific episode of care. The model is designed with the expectation that patient summary is available.

extension.valueMarkdown: A narrative overview supports human understanding of the patient’s history when structured data is incomplete or heterogeneous. Systems must be capable of populating and processing narrative content; omission is acceptable where justified during conformance testing.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process the generatedNarrative element of the patientHistory section.

element.short: Narrative, potentially formatted, content of the whole section. In case the section includes structured information, it should also be included in generatedNarrative.
element.definition: Narrative, potentially formatted, content of the whole section. In case the section includes structured information, it should also be included in generatedNarrative.

extension.valueMarkdown: Past problems relevant to the current episode of care should be representable in a structured format. Systems must be capable of populating this section when such information is available; omission is acceptable where permanently out of scope and justified during conformance testing.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process the pastProblems element of the patientHistory section when it is present in the document.

element.short: A list of conditions of a patient that the patient suffered in the past or still suffers. Unlike diagnostic summary, medical history is not only a list of problems, but could contain broader description of the condition and its progress, details about treatment including medication and patient response to treatment. Past problem section (unlike the same section of the patient summary) should include only conditions that are important for continuity of care. This section, if provided, complements the diagnostic summary section of the discharge report.
element.definition: A list of conditions of a patient that the patient suffered in the past or still suffers. Unlike diagnostic summary, medical history is not only a list of problems, but could contain broader description of the condition and its progress, details about treatment including medication and patient response to treatment. Past problem section (unlike the same section of the patient summary) should include only conditions that are important for continuity of care. This section, if provided, complements the diagnostic summary section of the discharge report.

extension.valueMarkdown: Information about devices and implants in patient history may be relevant for continuity of care. Systems must be capable of populating this section when such information is available; omission is acceptable where permanently out of scope and justified during conformance testing.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process the devicesAndImplants element of the patientHistory section when it is present in the document.

element.short: Devices and implants in patient anamnesis.
element.definition: Devices and implants in patient anamnesis.

extension.valueMarkdown: Historical procedures relevant to the current episode of care should be representable in a structured format. Systems must be capable of populating this section when such information is available; omission is acceptable where permanently out of scope and justified during conformance testing.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process the procedures element of the patientHistory section when it is present in the document.

element.short: Procedures in patient anamnesis.
element.definition:
Historical procedures performed on or for a patient, relevant for the current encounter.
Examples include surgical procedures, diagnostic procedures, endoscopic procedures, biopsies, counselling, physiotherapy, personal support services, adult day care services, etc.

element.short: Free text notes by the health professional
element.definition: Free text notes by the health professional

extension.valueMarkdown: Course of encounter is a core component of the discharge report. Systems claiming conformance must be capable of populating this section for every document instance.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process structured information about the course of encounter.

element.short: Course of inpatient or outpatient encounter.
element.definition: Course of inpatient or outpatient encounter.

extension.valueMarkdown: A narrative summary of the course of encounter supports human understanding of the clinical course. Systems must be capable of populating this narrative when such information is available.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process the generatedNarrative element of the courseOfEncounter section.

element.short: Narrative, potentially formatted, content of the whole section.
element.definition: Narrative, potentially formatted, content of the whole section.

extension.valueMarkdown: A diagnostic summary is essential for continuity of care. Systems claiming conformance must be capable of populating this section when such information is available.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process the diagnosticSummary element of the courseOfEncounter section.

element.short: Problems that were treated or affected provisioning of care (diagnostics, therapy, nursing, monitoring) during the encounter. At least one treated problem should be marked. Other problems are recorded only if they are important for continuity of care (after discharge).
element.definition: Problems that were treated or affected provisioning of care (diagnostics, therapy, nursing, monitoring) during the encounter. At least one treated problem should be marked. Other problems are recorded only if they are important for continuity of care (after discharge).

extension.valueMarkdown: Procedures performed during the encounter are important for continuity of care. Systems must be capable of populating this section when such information is available.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process the procedures element of the courseOfEncounter section when it is present in the document.

element.short: Procedures performed during encounter which are significant for continuity of care, e.g. surgeries and other instrumental interventions (endoscopic, intravascular), chemotherapy, radiotherapy, purification methods (dialysis, hemoperfusion), circulation support methods (counterpulsation, etc.), administration of blood derivatives or others. Diagnostic procedures are typically captured as observation results and not repeated here.
element.definition: Procedures performed during encounter which are significant for continuity of care, e.g. surgeries and other instrumental interventions (endoscopic, intravascular), chemotherapy, radiotherapy, purification methods (dialysis, hemoperfusion), circulation support methods (counterpulsation, etc.), administration of blood derivatives or others. Diagnostic procedures are typically captured as observation results and not repeated here.

extension.valueMarkdown: Devices and implants are clinically significant historical data and must be representable when medical history is present. Systems must be capable of populating and processing these entries.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process the medicalDevicesAndImplants element of the courseOfEncounter section when it is present in the document.

element.short: Medical devices (including implants) whose use was started, stopped, or otherwise modified during the encounter.
element.definition: Medical devices (including implants) whose use was started, stopped, or otherwise modified during the encounter.

extension.valueMarkdown: Medication treatments during the encounter are important for continuity of care. Systems must be capable of populating this section when such information is available.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process the medications element of the courseOfEncounter section when it is present in the document.

element.short: Significant medication treatments during encounter. The content of this section may be repeated in the medication summary for full overview of patient's medications.
element.definition: Significant medication treatments during encounter. The content of this section may be repeated in the medication summary for full overview of patient's medications.

extension.valueMarkdown: Test results of functional, diagnostic, and imaging examinations are important for continuity of care. Systems must be capable of populating this section when such information is available.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process the testResults element of the courseOfEncounter section when it is present in the document.

element.short: Significant medical test results of functional, diagnostic (including laboratory), and imaging examinations performed during encounter. This may include orders for which the results have not yet arrived.
element.definition: Significant medical test results of functional, diagnostic (including laboratory), and imaging examinations performed during encounter. This may include orders for which the results have not yet arrived.

element.short: Free text notes by the health professional
element.definition: Free text notes by the health professional

extension.valueMarkdown: Discharge details are essential to ensure safe transition of care and continuity after discharge. Systems claiming conformance must be capable of populating this section.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process structured information about discharge details.

element.short: Discharge details
element.definition: Structured information should be provided, however if not available, at least a section narrative should be present.

extension.valueMarkdown: Narrative discharge summaries support human understanding of discharge context. Systems must be capable of populating this narrative; omission is acceptable where justified during conformance testing.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process the generatedNarrative element of the dischargeDetails section.

element.short: Narrative, potentially formatted, content of the whole section.
element.definition: Narrative, potentially formatted, content of the whole section.

extension.valueMarkdown: Objective findings at discharge may not always be collected but must be representable when available. Systems must be capable of populating this section; omission is acceptable where justified during conformance testing.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process the objectiveFindings element of the dischargeDetails section when it is present in the document.

element.short: Objective findings, such as anthropometric measurements, vital signs, or objective anatomical findings of physical examination
element.definition: Objective findings, such as anthropometric measurements, vital signs, or objective anatomical findings of physical examination

extension.valueMarkdown: Functional status at discharge may influence care planning but is context-dependent. Systems must be capable of representing this section; omission is acceptable where justified during conformance testing.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process the functionalStatus element of the dischargeDetails section when it is present in the document.

element.short: An individual's ability to perform normal daily activities required to meet basic needs, fulfil usual roles and maintain health and well-being
element.definition: An individual's ability to perform normal daily activities required to meet basic needs, fulfil usual roles and maintain health and well-being

element.short: Free text notes by the health professional
element.definition: Free text notes by the health professional

extension.valueMarkdown: Medication summary at discharge may vary by encounter but must be representable when applicable. Systems must be capable of populating this section; omission is acceptable where justified during conformance testing.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process structured information about medication summary.

element.short: Summary information on the medication recommended for the period after discharge, indicating whether the medication is changed or newly started. Compared to previous practices, the overview is supplemented with medication that has been discontinued.
element.definition: Summary information on the medication recommended for the period after discharge, indicating whether the medication is changed or newly started. Compared to previous practices, the overview is supplemented with medication that has been discontinued.

extension.valueMarkdown: Narrative summarisation supports understanding of medication changes after discharge. Systems must be capable of populating this narrative; omission is acceptable where justified during conformance testing.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process the generatedNarrative element of the medicationSummary section.

element.short: Narrative, potentially formatted, content of the whole section.
element.definition: Narrative, potentially formatted, content of the whole section.

extension.valueMarkdown: Medication and dosing details are essential for safe post-discharge therapy. Systems must be capable of populating this information whenever a medication entry is present.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process the medicationUse element of the medicationSummary section when it is present in the document.

element.short: Details about medication and dosage
element.definition: Details about medication and dosage

element.short: Free text notes by the health professional
element.definition: Free text notes by the health professional

extension.valueMarkdown: Post-discharge care plans and recommendations may vary by encounter. Systems must be capable of representing this section; omission is acceptable where justified during conformance testing.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process structured information about care plan and recommendations after discharge.

element.short: Care plan and other recommendations after discharge.
element.definition: Care plan and other recommendations after discharge.

extension.valueMarkdown: Narrative recommendations support human interpretation of post-discharge care. Systems must be capable of populating this narrative; omission is acceptable where justified during conformance testing.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process the generatedNarrative element of the carePlan section.

element.short: Narrative, potentially formatted, content of the whole section.
element.definition: Narrative, potentially formatted, content of the whole section.

element.short: Structured care plan after discharge. Multiple care plans could be provided.
element.definition: Structured care plan after discharge. Multiple care plans could be provided.

extension.valueMarkdown: Additional advice may be clinically relevant but is context-dependent. Systems must be capable of representing this information; omission is acceptable where justified during conformance testing.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process the otherRecommendations element of the carePlan section when it is present in the document.

element.short: Other recommendations (advice) after discharge. E.g., recommendation to suggest hip replacement, reduce number of cigarettes, stop smoking, increase physical exercises, etc.
element.definition: Other recommendations (advice) after discharge. E.g., recommendation to suggest hip replacement, reduce number of cigarettes, stop smoking, increase physical exercises, etc.

element.short: Clinical synthesis (e.g. description of reasons and course of encounter) clustered by managed conditions. Clinical synthesis may include clinical reasoning (differential diagnostics, explanation of clinical context) in clinically complex conditions.
element.definition: Clinical synthesis (e.g. description of reasons and course of encounter) clustered by managed conditions. Clinical synthesis may include clinical reasoning (differential diagnostics, explanation of clinical context) in clinically complex conditions.

element.short: Report attachments. Implementations may define what kind of attachments are allowed and may ignore unexpected attachments for security reasons.
element.definition: Report attachments. Implementations may define what kind of attachments are allowed and may ignore unexpected attachments for security reasons.

extension.valueMarkdown: A common document header is necessary to ensure that the document can be unambiguously identified, associated with a patient, and processed in an exchange context. Systems claiming conformance must be capable of populating this metadata for every document instance.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process document header information.
EHR systems SHALL provide information about the subject of care.
EHR system SHALL render information about the subject of care in such a way that unambiguous assignment to the care subject is preserved.
Information about the subject of care SHALL be processed by the EHR system in such a way that the identity of the subject of care cannot be confused.

Systems claiming DR conformance shall demonstrate the capability to generate a human-readable representation of the document when required by the exchange context. This obligation applies to capability, not to population in every instance.
EHR systems that consume DR report SHALL be able to process the human readable version of the report.

Each EHR system that is a producer of a discharge report SHALL be able to provide structured information about the report body. This obligation applies to capability, not to population in every instance.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process structured body information.

Alerts may not be present in all encounters, but the document structure must support their representation. Systems must be capable of populating and processing this section when alerts are present; omission is acceptable where permanently out of scope and justified during conformance testing.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process the alerts section when it is present in the document.

Narrative representation supports immediate clinical understanding of alerts. Systems must be capable of populating this narrative; omission is acceptable where permanently out of scope and justified during conformance testing.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process the generatedNarrative element of the alerts section when it is present in the document.

Structured or textual alert entries represent clinically critical information when present. Systems must be capable of populating these entries; omission is acceptable where permanently out of scope and justified during conformance testing.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process the medicalAlert element of the alerts section when it is present in the document.
Encounter context is essential for interpreting all clinical content in the document. Systems claiming conformance must be capable of populating this section for every document instance.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process structured information about the encounter.
A narrative description of the encounter context is necessary to ensure human interpretability. Systems must be capable of populating this narrative in every document instance.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process the generatedNarrative element of the encounterInformation section.

A structured encounter reference supports linkage and reuse where available. Systems must be capable of populating this reference; omission is acceptable where permanently out of scope and justified during conformance testing.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process the encounter element of the encounterInformation section.

Admission evaluation does not occur in all encounters but must be representable where applicable. Systems must be capable of populating this section; omission is acceptable where permanently out of scope and justified during conformance testing.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process structured information about the admission evaluation.

Narrative summarisation supports clinical understanding of admission findings. Systems must be capable of populating this narrative; omission is acceptable where permanently out of scope and justified during conformance testing.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process the generatedNarrative element of the admissionEvaluation section.

Objective findings are part of structured clinical assessment when performed. Systems must be capable of populating this container; omission is acceptable where permanently out of scope and justified during conformance testing.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process the objectiveFindings element of the admissionEvaluation section when it is present in the document.

Functional status assessment is context-dependent but relevant for continuity of care. Systems must be capable of populating this section; omission is acceptable where permanently out of scope and justified during conformance testing.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process the functionalStatus element of the admissionEvaluation section when it is present in the document.

Patient history may not be available or relevant in all care contexts, but systems must be capable of representing this section when such information exists. Omission is acceptable where the capability is permanently out of scope and justified during conformance testing.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process structured information about patient history.

A narrative overview supports human understanding of the patient’s history when structured data is incomplete or heterogeneous. Systems must be capable of populating and processing narrative content; omission is acceptable where justified during conformance testing.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process the generatedNarrative element of the patientHistory section.

Past problems relevant to the current episode of care should be representable in a structured format. Systems must be capable of populating this section when such information is available; omission is acceptable where permanently out of scope and justified during conformance testing.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process the pastProblems element of the patientHistory section when it is present in the document.

Information about devices and implants in patient history may be relevant for continuity of care. Systems must be capable of populating this section when such information is available; omission is acceptable where permanently out of scope and justified during conformance testing.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process the devicesAndImplants element of the patientHistory section when it is present in the document.

Historical procedures relevant to the current episode of care should be representable in a structured format. Systems must be capable of populating this section when such information is available; omission is acceptable where permanently out of scope and justified during conformance testing.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process the procedures element of the patientHistory section when it is present in the document.
Course of encounter is a core component of the discharge report. Systems claiming conformance must be capable of populating this section for every document instance.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process structured information about the course of encounter.
A narrative summary of the course of encounter supports human understanding of the clinical course. Systems must be capable of populating this narrative when such information is available.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process the generatedNarrative element of the courseOfEncounter section.
A diagnostic summary is essential for continuity of care. Systems claiming conformance must be capable of populating this section when such information is available.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process the diagnosticSummary element of the courseOfEncounter section.
Procedures performed during the encounter are important for continuity of care. Systems must be capable of populating this section when such information is available.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process the procedures element of the courseOfEncounter section when it is present in the document.
Devices and implants are clinically significant historical data and must be representable when medical history is present. Systems must be capable of populating and processing these entries.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process the medicalDevicesAndImplants element of the courseOfEncounter section when it is present in the document.
Medication treatments during the encounter are important for continuity of care. Systems must be capable of populating this section when such information is available.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process the medications element of the courseOfEncounter section when it is present in the document.
Test results of functional, diagnostic, and imaging examinations are important for continuity of care. Systems must be capable of populating this section when such information is available.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process the testResults element of the courseOfEncounter section when it is present in the document.
Discharge details are essential to ensure safe transition of care and continuity after discharge. Systems claiming conformance must be capable of populating this section.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process structured information about discharge details.

Narrative discharge summaries support human understanding of discharge context. Systems must be capable of populating this narrative; omission is acceptable where justified during conformance testing.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process the generatedNarrative element of the dischargeDetails section.

Objective findings at discharge may not always be collected but must be representable when available. Systems must be capable of populating this section; omission is acceptable where justified during conformance testing.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process the objectiveFindings element of the dischargeDetails section when it is present in the document.

Functional status at discharge may influence care planning but is context-dependent. Systems must be capable of representing this section; omission is acceptable where justified during conformance testing.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process the functionalStatus element of the dischargeDetails section when it is present in the document.

Medication summary at discharge may vary by encounter but must be representable when applicable. Systems must be capable of populating this section; omission is acceptable where justified during conformance testing.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process structured information about medication summary.

Narrative summarisation supports understanding of medication changes after discharge. Systems must be capable of populating this narrative; omission is acceptable where justified during conformance testing.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process the generatedNarrative element of the medicationSummary section.
Medication and dosing details are essential for safe post-discharge therapy. Systems must be capable of populating this information whenever a medication entry is present.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process the medicationUse element of the medicationSummary section when it is present in the document.
Post-discharge care plans and recommendations may vary by encounter. Systems must be capable of representing this section; omission is acceptable where justified during conformance testing.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process structured information about care plan and recommendations after discharge.
Narrative recommendations support human interpretation of post-discharge care. Systems must be capable of populating this narrative; omission is acceptable where justified during conformance testing.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process the generatedNarrative element of the carePlan section.
Additional advice may be clinically relevant but is context-dependent. Systems must be capable of representing this information; omission is acceptable where justified during conformance testing.
EHR systems that consume discharge report SHALL process the otherRecommendations element of the carePlan section when it is present in the document.
